columns.extraction_plan: The add-on treatment is "Abiraterone acetate plus prednisolone". This is explicitly stated in the title and abstract, and further detailed in the "Procedures" section.

The total number of participants in the control arm (SOC-alone) is reported as 502. This can be found in: Figure 1 (CONSORT diagram). Locate the box "Allocated to SOC" and extract the value "N = 502".

The median age for the treatment arm (SOC + AAP) is reported in Table 1, "Baseline characteristics", on page 5. Locate the row "Age (years)" and the sub-row "Median (IQR)". The value in the column "SOC + AAP" is "67 (62-71)".

The median age for the control arm (SOC-alone) is reported in Table 1, "Baseline characteristics", on page 5. Locate the row "Age (years)" and the sub-row "Median (IQR)". The value in the column "SOC-alone" is "67 (62-72)".

The control arm is referred to as "SOC-alone" or "SOC" (Standard-of-Care alone). This is explicitly stated in the abstract and methods section. From the abstract (Chunk 5, page 3, "CANCER THERAPY AND PREVENTION" section): "The comparison randomised patients 1:1 to SOC-alone with or without daily abiraterone acetate 1000 mg + prednisolone 5 mg (SOC + AAP), continued until disease progression." From the "Randomisation and masking" section (Chunk 6, page 4): "Allocation was 1:1 to standard-of-care (SOC-alone) only group or SOC-alone with abiraterone acetate and prednisolone/prednisone group (SOC + AAP)." The tables (Table 1 on page 5, Table 2 on page 6, Table 3 on page 8) and figures (Figure 2 on page 7, Figure 3 on page 9) also use "SOC-alone" or "SOC" to denote the control group.

On page 8, in the "DISCUSSION" section, the text states: "Reported use of docetaxel within 1 year after first progression was higher among patients allocated to SOC + AAP (32% SOC-alone and 40% SOC + AAP, Chi-square P = .048)." The treatment arm is "SOC + AAP".

On page 8, in the "DISCUSSION" section, the text states: "Reported use of docetaxel within 1 year after first progression was higher among patients allocated to SOC + AAP (32% SOC-alone and 40% SOC + AAP, Chi-square P = .048)." The control arm is "SOC-alone".

The PDF describes the trial design and execution, including aspects relevant to risk of bias (e.g., "There was no blinding to treatment allocation for practical reasons"), but it does not provide an explicit "overall risk judgment of individual criteria of evidence" using a structured framework (e.g., low/high/unclear risk of bias for specific domains).

The median follow-up duration is reported in the "RESULTS" section. Extract the value "73" from the sentence: "The database was locked for this analysis on 3 April 2020. Median follow-up was 73 months (6.1 years)."

However, the STAMPEDE trial is described as a "multiarm, multistage platform trial" (Chunk 5, page 2, Abstract) and the CONSORT diagram (Chunk 1, page 5, Figure 1) shows patients allocated to "other treatment groups" (N = 835), indicating more than two arms in the overall trial. The total count of all arms in the entire STAMPEDE trial is not explicitly stated as a numerical value.

The primary outcome measure is "overall survival". This is explicitly stated in the Abstract on page 3: "The primary outcome measure was overall survival." and reiterated in Section 2.4 "Outcome measures and statistical analysis" on page 4: "The primary outcome for this comparison was overall survival...".

The progression-free survival rate for the treatment arm (SOC + AAP) is reported in Table 2, under the "Progression-free survival" section, as "% event-free at 5 years (95% CI)". The value is 54% (50-59). The hazard ratio for SOC + AAP versus SOC-alone is 0.58 (95% CI: 0.49-0.69), P < .0001.

The progression-free survival rate for the control arm (SOC-alone) is reported in Table 2, under the "Progression-free survival" section, as "% event-free at 5 years (95% CI)". The value is 37% (33-42). The hazard ratio for SOC + AAP versus SOC-alone is 0.58 (95% CI: 0.49-0.69), P < .0001.

The specific Quality of Life Scale used or its results are not reported in this PDF for the abiraterone comparison. The "Outcome measures and statistical analysis" section (page 4, text) does not list Quality of Life as a primary or secondary outcome for this analysis.

columns.extraction_plan: The name of the experimental arm is "SOC + AAP". This is explicitly stated in the abstract and consistently used throughout the document to refer to the abiraterone acetate plus prednisolone arm.

The number of participants in the SOC-alone arm is reported as 502. This can be found in Table 1, titled "Baseline characteristics", on page 5, under the column "SOC-alone n = 502". It is also visible in Figure 1, the CONSORT diagram, on page 5, as "Allocated to SOC N = 502". The number of participants in the SOC + AAP arm is reported as 501. The document classifies patients into "High-risk" and "Low-risk" groups using LATITUDE criteria (page 4, section 2.3). The text on page 8 states that the LATITUDE and CHAARTED systems for defining metastatic disease risk group "largely coincided" and that "There was no evidence that the classifier used affected the overall conclusion". Therefore, the "High-risk" data from Table 3 is used. The trial name is reported in the title of the article. Extract "STAMPEDE randomised trial (NCT00268476)" from the text on page 1.

The treatment classes are "Standard-of-care (SOC)" and "Standard-of-care plus abiraterone acetate and prednisolone (SOC + AAP)". This information is explicitly stated in the abstract and the "Procedures" section.

The type of therapy is described in the abstract and the methods section. From the abstract on page 1: "Abiraterone acetate plus prednisolone for metastatic patients starting hormone therapy". From the abstract on page 2: "Standard-of-care (SOC) was androgen deprivation therapy." and "Abiraterone acetate plus prednisolone (AAP) previously demonstrated improved survival in STAMPEDE, a multiarm, multistage platform trial in men starting long-term hormone therapy for prostate cancer." From Section 2.3 "Procedures" on page 4: "All patients received lifelong ADT using gonadotrophin-releasing hormone (GnRH) agonists, antagonists, or orchidectomy. Patients allocated to the SOC + AAP group were also planned to receive abiraterone (1000 mg daily) with prednisolone (5 mg daily)." Value: Standard-of-care (SOC) was androgen deprivation therapy (ADT) using gonadotrophin-releasing hormone (GnRH) agonists, antagonists, or orchidectomy. The experimental arm (SOC + AAP) received SOC plus abiraterone acetate (1000 mg daily) and prednisolone (5 mg daily).
